title_pl: sin_via_cos: ∃ t, ∀ x ≠ π/2, vc.re = sin x via subst0
title_en: sin_via_cos: ∃ t, ∀ x ≠ π/2, vc.re = sin x via subst0
paper_quote: GPT Pro consult §2: sin via cos(π/2 - x) reuses full-domain cosTermℂ
informal_pl: Świadek sinViaCosℂ przez subst0 cosTermℂ_full halfPiMinusXℂ; rzut .re przez Real.cos_pi_div_two_sub.
informal_en: sinViaCosℂ = cosTermℂ_full.subst0 halfPiMinusXℂ; .re projection via Real.cos_pi_div_two_sub.
lean_target_signature: theorem sin_via_cos_correct (x : ℝ) (hx : x ≠ Real.pi / 2) : ∃ vc : ℂ, ... ∧ vc.re = Real.sin x
notes: SEALED 2026-05-08 by Aristotle, ~21 min. Strategy: rewrite via def, apply eval?_subst0 + eval?_halfPiMinusXℂ, derive π/2 - x ≠ 0 via sub_ne_zero, apply cosTermℂ_full_correct, finish with Real.cos_pi_div_two_sub. Note: framework lift requires sin_full_witness_family using cos_full_witness_family (since cosTermℂ_full is a witness FAMILY in our actual code, not a single term).
